incorrects.description: Student selected 6, the number of symbols instead of the total value

steps.text: You counted 6 symbols. But each symbol stands for 5 students. Look at the key. What does each symbol represent?

incorrects.description: Student selected 25, stopped skip-counting one step too early

steps.text: You're using skip-counting. Count all 6 symbols by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, and one more. What comes next?

incorrects.description: Student selected 35, counted one symbol too many

steps.text: You're skip-counting by 5s. Count the symbols in the Water column carefully. How many symbols do you see?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Water column highlights. System counts each symbol: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30.

steps.text: Let me show you. Water has 6 symbols. Each symbol represents 5 students. I count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. Six symbols at a scale of 5 equals 30 students. The key tells you what each symbol is worth, then you skip-count to find the total.
